_readme: Edit this file to add or remove import sources. Run `npm run import` after editing. githubRepos are scanned recursively for .pdf/.md files and auto-classified into existing Subjects by keyword matching (see src/services/githubImporter.service.js). externalResources are single direct links to openly-licensed content that isn't structured as a GitHub repo (e.g. OpenStax). Subjects/Semesters/Courses referenced here must already exist - run `npm run seed` first.

githubRepos.attribution: Open Source Society University (OSSU) - Computer Science curriculum, github.com/ossu/computer-science

githubRepos.license: UNVERIFIED - confirm license before importing at scale
githubRepos.attribution: OpenDSA Project, github.com/OpenDSA/OpenDSA - verify redistribution terms; content historically CC BY-NC-SA which may restrict commercial-adjacent use

githubRepos.license: UNVERIFIED - no LICENSE file confirmed at time of writing
githubRepos.attribution: muhammadanas05/University-Notes on GitHub - CONFIRM LICENSE before importing publicly. Do not run this source until you've checked the repo for a LICENSE file or contacted the author.

externalResources.title: Anatomy & Physiology 2e (OpenStax)
externalResources.attribution: Anatomy and Physiology 2e, OpenStax - openstax.org, licensed CC BY 4.0

externalResources.title: Anatomy & Physiology 2e (OpenStax) - Physiology Units
externalResources.attribution: Anatomy and Physiology 2e, OpenStax - openstax.org, licensed CC BY 4.0

_todo: EnterMedSchool.org publishes CC-licensed medical glossary/illustration content and links a GitHub repo from its site ('View on GitHub' button), but the exact repo URL wasn't confirmed while building this config. Visit https://www.entermedschool.org/en, find that link, and add it as a githubRepos entry with courseSlug 'medicine-and-surgery' once confirmed.

TeachMeSeries (TeachMeAnatomy, TeachMeSurgery, etc.) and Geeky Medics have excellent, well-explained clinical notes but are NOT confirmed to be openly licensed for redistribution. Do not scrape/import them under this import-only architecture - if you want to reference them, add them as manual 'link' type Resource documents that point students to the original site instead of hosting their content.
